---
title: "ubiProt"
output: html_document
date: "2025-02-25"
---

UbiProt Scatter Plots
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Load the ubiquitomics results ---
dep_ubi_MGH_new_results <- readRDS(file = "Ubi_dep_MGH_BH_new.rds")
dep_ubi_8330_new_results <- readRDS(file = "Ubi_dep_8330_BH_new.rds")

# --- Load the proteomics results ---
dep_pro_MGH_new_results <- readRDS(file = "dep_MGH_BH_new.rds")
dep_pro_8330_new_results <- readRDS(file = "dep_8330_BH_new.rds")

# ==============================================================================
# STEP 1: DEFINE THE COMMON UBIQUITOME UNIVERSE
# ==============================================================================

# Extract gene names
genes_ubi_MGH <- rowData(dep_ubi_MGH_new_results)$name
genes_ubi_8330 <- rowData(dep_ubi_8330_new_results)$name

# Clean names (uppercase + trim) to ensure matches
genes_ubi_MGH_clean <- trimws(toupper(genes_ubi_MGH))
genes_ubi_8330_clean <- trimws(toupper(genes_ubi_8330))

# Find intersection: Proteins present in ALL ubiquitome datasets
common_ubi_genes <- intersect(genes_ubi_MGH_clean, genes_ubi_8330_clean)

# Print the list count
cat("\n------------------------------------------------------------\n")
cat("PROTEIN COUNT SUMMARY:\n")
cat("Proteins in MGH Ubiquitome: ", length(genes_ubi_MGH_clean), "\n")
cat("Proteins in 8330 Ubiquitome:", length(genes_ubi_8330_clean), "\n")
cat("Proteins present in ALL Ubiquitome datasets (Intersection):", length(common_ubi_genes), "\n")
cat("------------------------------------------------------------\n\n")


# ==============================================================================
# STEP 2: SCATTER PLOT FUNCTION
# ==============================================================================

create_scatter_plot <- function(condition) {
  
  # --- Extract data for MGH background ---
  ubi_fc_MGH <- rowData(dep_ubi_MGH_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_diff")]
  pro_fc_MGH <- rowData(dep_pro_MGH_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_diff")]
  ubi_genes_MGH <- rowData(dep_ubi_MGH_new_results)$name
  pro_genes_MGH <- rowData(dep_pro_MGH_new_results)$name
  ubi_pvals_MGH <- rowData(dep_ubi_MGH_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_p.adj")]
  pro_pvals_MGH <- rowData(dep_pro_MGH_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_p.adj")]
  
  # Create DF
  ubi_df_MGH <- data.frame(gene = toupper(trimws(ubi_genes_MGH)), fc = ubi_fc_MGH, pval = ubi_pvals_MGH)
  pro_df_MGH <- data.frame(gene = toupper(trimws(pro_genes_MGH)), fc = pro_fc_MGH, pval = pro_pvals_MGH)
  
  # --- Extract data for 8330 background ---
  ubi_fc_8330 <- rowData(dep_ubi_8330_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_diff")]
  pro_fc_8330 <- rowData(dep_pro_8330_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_diff")]
  ubi_genes_8330 <- rowData(dep_ubi_8330_new_results)$name
  pro_genes_8330 <- rowData(dep_pro_8330_new_results)$name
  ubi_pvals_8330 <- rowData(dep_ubi_8330_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_p.adj")]
  pro_pvals_8330 <- rowData(dep_pro_8330_new_results)[, paste0(condition, "_vs_WT_p.adj")]
  
  # Create DF
  ubi_df_8330 <- data.frame(gene = toupper(trimws(ubi_genes_8330)), fc = ubi_fc_8330, pval = ubi_pvals_8330)
  pro_df_8330 <- data.frame(gene = toupper(trimws(pro_genes_8330)), fc = pro_fc_8330, pval = pro_pvals_8330)
  
  # --- FILTER: Keep only genes in our Common Ubiquitome Universe ---
  ubi_df_MGH <- ubi_df_MGH %>% dplyr::filter(gene %in% common_ubi_genes)
  ubi_df_8330 <- ubi_df_8330 %>% dplyr::filter(gene %in% common_ubi_genes)
  
  # --- Merge with Proteomics (Intersection) ---
  # Note: This will drop proteins that are in the Ubiquitome universe but MISSING in proteomics
  merged_df_MGH <- merge(ubi_df_MGH, pro_df_MGH, by = "gene", suffixes = c("_ubi_MGH", "_pro_MGH"))
  merged_df_8330 <- merge(ubi_df_8330, pro_df_8330, by = "gene", suffixes = c("_ubi_8330", "_pro_8330"))
  
  # --- Merge Backgrounds ---
  merged_df <- merge(merged_df_MGH, merged_df_8330, by = "gene")
  
  # --- Print Final Count for this Plot ---
  cat(paste0("Plot '", condition, "': ", nrow(merged_df), " proteins (Common Ubi + Common Pro)\n"))
  
  # --- Significance Logic ---
  merged_df$significant <- ifelse(
    (merged_df$pval_ubi_MGH < 0.1 & merged_df$pval_ubi_8330 < 0.1),
    "p-adj. < 0.05 in both backgrounds (ubiquitomics)",
    "no"
  )
  
  # --- Labeling ---
  proteins_to_label <- c("NDN","CALM1;CALM2;CALM3", "ANXA6", "RHOA", "TAGLN3", "LMNB1", "CALR", "PRSS3", "LIN28A")
  merged_df$label <- ifelse(merged_df$gene %in% proteins_to_label, merged_df$gene, "")
  
  # --- Plotting ---
  p <- ggplot(merged_df, aes(x = fc_pro_MGH, y = fc_ubi_MGH, color = significant)) +
    geom_point(data = merged_df[merged_df$significant == "no", ], color = "#ebebeb", size = 2) +
    geom_point(data = merged_df[merged_df$significant == "p-adj. < 0.05 in both backgrounds (ubiquitomics)", ], color = "#c0c0c0", size = 2) +
    geom_point(data = merged_df[merged_df$label != "", ], color = "#b34747", size = 2) +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), data = merged_df[merged_df$label != "", ], color = "#b34747",
                    size = 3, box.padding = 0.5, point.padding = 0.5, max.overlaps = 20) +
    labs(
      x = "Log2 Fold Change (Proteomics)",
      y = "Log2 Fold Change (Ubiquitomics)",
      title = paste0(condition, " vs WT Fold Changes (MGH and 8330 Backgrounds)"),
      color = "" 
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "#c0c0c0", size = 3, shape = 19))) +
    xlim(c(-5, 5)) + ylim(c(-4, 4))
  
  return(p)
}

# --- Create scatter plots for each condition ---
conditions <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")
for (condition in conditions) {
  print(create_scatter_plot(condition))
}
```


Proteins that are in the ubiquitine data set but not the proteome one
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# INVESTIGATING THE "UBIQUITOME-ONLY" PROTEINS
# ==============================================================================

# --- 1. Re-Define the Common Ubiquitome (5,039 proteins) ---
genes_ubi_MGH <- rowData(dep_ubi_MGH_new_results)$name
genes_ubi_8330 <- rowData(dep_ubi_8330_new_results)$name
common_ubi_genes <- intersect(trimws(toupper(genes_ubi_MGH)), trimws(toupper(genes_ubi_8330)))

# --- 2. Define the Common Proteome ---
genes_pro_MGH <- rowData(dep_pro_MGH_new_results)$name
genes_pro_8330 <- rowData(dep_pro_8330_new_results)$name
# We look for proteins present in EITHER proteome dataset to be safe, 
# or strictly BOTH if you want to match your previous strict filtering.
# Based on your plot logic, you required them in ALL datasets, so let's check that.
common_pro_genes <- intersect(trimws(toupper(genes_pro_MGH)), trimws(toupper(genes_pro_8330)))

# --- 3. Find the Difference (Ubi ONLY) ---
# Which genes are in 'common_ubi' but NOT in 'common_pro'?
ubi_only_genes <- setdiff(common_ubi_genes, common_pro_genes)

# --- 4. Output the Results ---
cat("\n------------------------------------------------------------\n")
cat("MISSING PROTEIN ANALYSIS\n")
cat("------------------------------------------------------------\n")
cat("Total Common Ubiquitome Genes: ", length(common_ubi_genes), "\n")
cat("Total Common Proteome Genes:   ", length(common_pro_genes), "\n")
cat("\n")
cat("Proteins found in Ubiquitome but MISSING in Proteome: ", length(ubi_only_genes), "\n")
cat("------------------------------------------------------------\n")

# Print the list (first 200)
if(length(ubi_only_genes) > 0) {
  print(head(ubi_only_genes, 200))
  
  # Optional: Write to file if list is huge
  # write.csv(ubi_only_genes, "Proteins_Unique_to_Ubiquitome.csv")
}
```



Ridgeline Plots Ubiquitome-Proteome-Ratio
```{r}
# --- Step 1: Load Libraries ---
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(openxlsx)
library(ggridges)
library(tidyr) # Ensure tidyr is loaded for pivot/gather if needed

# --- Step 2: Setup Themes & Colors ---
gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
  hues <- seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
  hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
}
condition_order <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII", "WT")
genotype_colors <- gg_color_hue(length(condition_order))
names(genotype_colors) <- condition_order

# Updated Theme
publication_theme <- theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# --- Step 3: Load Data ---
abundance_ubi <- read.xlsx(xlsxFile = "./Ubiquitination_IP_Azmal.xlsx")
abundance_ubi <- abundance_ubi[!duplicated(abundance_ubi$PG.Genes) & !is.na(abundance_ubi$PG.Genes), ]
rownames(abundance_ubi) <- toupper(trimws(abundance_ubi$PG.Genes))

abundance_pro <- read.xlsx(xlsxFile = "./Final_Full_Proteome_Azmal.xlsx")
abundance_pro <- abundance_pro[!duplicated(abundance_pro$PG.Genes) & !is.na(abundance_pro$PG.Genes), ]
rownames(abundance_pro) <- toupper(trimws(abundance_pro$PG.Genes))

common_genes <- intersect(rownames(abundance_ubi), rownames(abundance_pro))

# --- Step 4: Robust Ratio Calculation Function ---
calculate_ratio_robust <- function(condition, ubi_data, pro_data, ubi_prefix, pro_prefix_main, pro_prefix_wt) {
  
  # 1. Identify Columns
  ubi_condition_pattern <- case_when(
    condition == "DelC" ~ "Del", condition == "DupC" ~ "DUPC",
    condition == "HetDel" ~ "HET", condition == "HomoDel" ~ "HOM",
    condition == "PWSTI" ~ "PWS1", condition == "PWSTII" ~ "PWS2",
    TRUE ~ condition
  )
  
  ubi_cols <- grep(paste0(ubi_prefix, ubi_condition_pattern), colnames(ubi_data), value = TRUE)
  pro_cols <- if (condition == "WT") grep(pro_prefix_wt, colnames(pro_data), value = TRUE) else grep(paste0(pro_prefix_main, condition), colnames(pro_data), value = TRUE)
  
  if (length(ubi_cols) == 0 || length(pro_cols) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Extract Data
  ubi_vals <- sapply(ubi_data[common_genes, ubi_cols, drop = FALSE], as.numeric)
  pro_vals <- sapply(pro_data[common_genes, pro_cols, drop = FALSE], as.numeric)
  
  ubi_mean <- rowMeans(ubi_vals, na.rm = TRUE)
  pro_mean <- rowMeans(pro_vals, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # 3. Auto-Detect Scale (Raw vs Log2) based on Max Value
  is_raw_ubi <- max(ubi_mean, na.rm = TRUE) > 100
  
  ratio_df <- data.frame(gene = common_genes, ubi_mean, pro_mean) %>%
    dplyr::filter(ubi_mean > 0, pro_mean > 0) # Remove zeros
  
  # 4. Calculate Log2 Ratio
  if (is_raw_ubi) {
    # Data is RAW -> Divide then Log
    ratio_df$log2_ratio <- log2(ratio_df$ubi_mean / ratio_df$pro_mean)
  } else {
    # Data is LOG -> Subtract
    ratio_df$log2_ratio <- ratio_df$ubi_mean - ratio_df$pro_mean
  }
  
  ratio_df$condition <- condition
  return(ratio_df)
}

# --- Step 5: Generate Data ---
mgh_ratio_data <- lapply(condition_order, calculate_ratio_robust,
                         ubi_data = abundance_ubi, pro_data = abundance_pro,
                         ubi_prefix = "IP_M_", pro_prefix_main = "FP_MG_", pro_prefix_wt = "FP_M_WT") %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  mutate(condition = factor(condition, levels = rev(condition_order)))

h8330_ratio_data <- lapply(condition_order, calculate_ratio_robust,
                           ubi_data = abundance_ubi, pro_data = abundance_pro,
                           ubi_prefix = "IP_G_", pro_prefix_main = "FP_GM_", pro_prefix_wt = "FP_GM_WT") %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  mutate(condition = factor(condition, levels = rev(condition_order)))


# --- Step 6: Plotting with Fixes ---

# FIX 1: Add padding to BOTH Bottom (0.2) and Top (0.5)
ridge_expansion <- expansion(mult = c(0.2, 0.5))

mgh_ridgeline_plot <- ggplot(mgh_ratio_data, aes(x = log2_ratio, y = condition, fill = condition, color = condition)) +
  # FIX 2: Use 'linewidth' instead of 'size'
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1.5, linewidth = 0.8) + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(mgh_ratio_data$log2_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genotype_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = genotype_colors) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = ridge_expansion) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Ubiquitome / Proteome Ratios (MGH)",
    x = bquote(Log[2]~"(Ubiquitome / Proteome)")
  ) +
  # FIX 3: Widen the zoom slightly (0.001 to 0.999) to ensure tails aren't cut
  coord_cartesian(xlim = quantile(mgh_ratio_data$log2_ratio, c(0.001, 0.999), na.rm = TRUE)) +
  publication_theme

h8330_ridgeline_plot <- ggplot(h8330_ratio_data, aes(x = log2_ratio, y = condition, fill = condition, color = condition)) +
  # FIX 2: Use 'linewidth' instead of 'size'
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1.5, linewidth = 0.8) + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(h8330_ratio_data$log2_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genotype_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = genotype_colors) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = ridge_expansion) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Ubiquitome / Proteome Ratios (8330)",
    x = bquote(Log[2]~"(Ubiquitome / Proteome)")
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = quantile(h8330_ratio_data$log2_ratio, c(0.001, 0.999), na.rm = TRUE)) +
  publication_theme

# --- Step 7: Display ---
print(mgh_ridgeline_plot)
print(h8330_ridgeline_plot)
```



Export results of ubiquitome-proteome ratio
```{r}
# Load library
library(openxlsx)

# ==============================================================================
# EXPORT: UBIQUITOME-PROTEOME RATIOS (2 Sheets per Genotype)
# ==============================================================================

# 1. Prepare and Combine Data
# Add Background column to distinguish them
mgh_ratio_data$Background <- "MGH"
h8330_ratio_data$Background <- "8330"

# Combine and rename columns for professional output
combined_data <- rbind(mgh_ratio_data, h8330_ratio_data) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Gene = gene,
    Background,
    Genotype = condition,
    Mean_Ubiquitome_Abundance = ubi_mean,
    Mean_Proteome_Abundance = pro_mean,
    Ubiquitome_Proteome_Log2_Ratio = log2_ratio
  ) %>%
  arrange(Background, Genotype, Gene)

# 2. Initialize Workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()
header_style <- createStyle(textDecoration = "bold", halign = "center")

# 3. Loop Logic: Create sheets for Genotype + Background
unique_genotypes <- unique(combined_data$Genotype)
unique_backgrounds <- c("MGH", "8330")

for (geno in unique_genotypes) {
  for (bg in unique_backgrounds) {
    
    # A. Filter data for this specific Genotype AND Background
    sheet_data <- combined_data %>% 
      filter(Genotype == geno, Background == bg) %>%
      dplyr::select(-Genotype, -Background) # Remove redundant columns (in sheet name)
    
    # Check if data exists
    if(nrow(sheet_data) > 0) {
      
      # B. Create Sheet Name (e.g., "DelC_MGH")
      sheet_name <- paste0(geno, "_", bg)
      sheet_name <- substr(sheet_name, 1, 31) # Max excel length
      
      addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
      
      # C. Write Data
      writeData(wb, sheet = sheet_name, x = sheet_data)
      
      # D. Apply Formatting
      addStyle(wb, sheet = sheet_name, style = header_style, rows = 1, cols = 1:ncol(sheet_data))
      setColWidths(wb, sheet = sheet_name, cols = 1:ncol(sheet_data), widths = "auto")
    }
  }
}

# 4. Save
saveWorkbook(wb, "Supplementary_Table_Ubi_Prot_Ratios.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

cat("\nSuccess! 'Supplementary_Table_Ubi_Prot_Ratios.xlsx' created.")
cat("\nContains sheets organized by Genotype_Background (e.g., 'DelC_MGH', 'PWSTI_8330').")
```
